skill.name: azure-monitor-opentelemetry-ts
skill.description: Instrument Node.js applications with Azure Monitor and OpenTelemetry for distributed tracing, metrics, and logs using the @azure/monitor-opentelemetry package.
skill.summary: Auto-instrument TypeScript and JavaScript applications with Azure Monitor telemetry using OpenTelemetry standards.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The scanner analyzed SKILL.md documentation as executable code. The skill provides legitimate Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry instrumentation guidance with standard environment variable configuration and documented network endpoints for telemetry export.

low_findings.title: Documentation Scanned as Code
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged SKILL.md documentation examples as security issues. All findings are false positives - the file contains TypeScript code examples and bash commands for documentation purposes only.

content.user_title: Add Azure Monitor Telemetry to Node.js Apps
content.value_statement: Application teams need observability for production debugging and performance monitoring. This skill provides complete guidance for instrumenting TypeScript and JavaScript applications with Azure Monitor using OpenTelemetry standards.
actual_capabilities: Configure auto-instrumentation for Node.js applications with Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry
Set up custom tracing with manual span creation and attribute recording
Create custom metrics including counters, histograms, and observable gauges
Configure trace, metric, and log exporters with manual exporter setup
Implement custom span processors for filtering and enrichment
Set up sampling ratios for high-traffic applications
limitations: Requires Azure Monitor connection string and Azure subscription
Auto-instrumentation requires Node.js 18.19+ for ESM loader support
Custom span processors must be implemented in TypeScript or JavaScript
Log ingestion requires separate @azure/monitor-ingestion package setup

use_cases.title: Production Application Monitoring
use_cases.description: Set up comprehensive telemetry for a production Node.js API to track request latencies, error rates, and dependencies.

use_cases.title: Microservices Distributed Tracing
use_cases.description: Enable end-to-end trace visibility across microservices using OpenTelemetry standard context propagation.

use_cases.title: Custom Business Metrics
use_cases.description: Track application-specific metrics like user actions, feature usage, and business KPIs alongside standard telemetry.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Auto-Instrumentation Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry to my Express.js application with default configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Trace Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to configure Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry with custom sampling, offline storage, and specific instrumentation options for my production app.

prompt_templates.title: Manual Span Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to track a specific operation with custom spans, attributes, and events. Show me how to create manual traces with proper error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Metrics and Processors
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up custom metrics with a meter provider, create a filtering span processor, and configure the log exporter for my application.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user building complete observability solution

output_examples.input: Add Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry to my Express app
output_examples.output: Install the package with npm install @azure/monitor-opentelemetry, then import and call useAzureMonitor with your connection string before importing Express. The SDK will automatically instrument HTTP requests, database calls, and Azure SDK operations.

output_examples.input: Create a custom span for my database operation
output_examples.output: Use trace.getTracer to get a tracer, call startSpan with operation name, set attributes like database type and query, add events for processing stages, record exceptions on errors, and call end in a finally block.

best_practices: Call useAzureMonitor before importing any other modules to ensure proper auto-instrumentation
Store connection strings in environment variables, never in source code
Implement graceful shutdown with shutdownAzureMonitor to flush pending telemetry
anti_patterns: Do not skip calling shutdownAzureMonitor on application exit - telemetry will be lost
Avoid setting sampling ratio to 0 in production - you will lose all telemetry data
Do not initialize useAzureMonitor multiple times - it causes resource conflicts

faq.question: What is the minimum Node.js version required?
faq.answer: Node.js 18.19.0 or later is required for ESM loader support. For CommonJS, Node.js 14+ is supported.

faq.question: Do I need to install separate exporters?
faq.answer: No, @azure/monitor-opentelemetry includes the exporters. Install @azure/monitor-opentelemetry-exporter separately only for custom low-level setups.

faq.question: How do I enable ESM loader?
faq.answer: Run your application with node --import @azure/monitor-opentelemetry/loader or add the import to your package.json scripts.

faq.question: Can I filter which traces are sent to Azure Monitor?
faq.answer: Yes, use custom span processors with onEnd to filter or modify spans, or configure the samplingRatio option.

faq.question: How do I add custom dimensions to all telemetry?
faq.answer: Use a custom span processor's onEnd method to add attributes to spans, or use resourceFromAttributes when configuring the SDK.

faq.question: What happens if Azure Monitor is unavailable?
faq.answer: Enable offline storage with the storageDirectory option to buffer telemetry locally and retry later.
